title: Ralph Phase L: concrete kernel wins — bfp8 KV cache + ternary_matmul fidelity probe

stories.title: Baseline confirmation on current HEAD (post-K1 landing)
stories.finding: Server at 7b282d8 (pulled cleanly). bench_batch32: decode_tps 74.35 t/s, p50 11.6ms, min 10.7ms — in range. Previous cycle bitnet.cpp PCC 0.9699 still valid.

stories.title: Speed lever: KV cache dtype bfp8 — DRAM bandwidth reduction
stories.finding: DEAD END. bfp8 KV cache regresses decode_tps 74.35 -> 24.57 t/s (3x slower) with max-latency spikes to 1005ms (vs baseline 91ms). The bfp8 quantization on every DRAM read in the SDPA_decode+paged_update_cache path introduces synchronization stalls. Reverted env flag; cache stays bf16.
acceptanceCriteria: Plumbed BITNET_KV_CACHE_DTYPE env through generator_batch32 preallocate call — DONE then REVERTED
bench_batch32 with cache_dtype=bfp8 — DONE (decode_tps 24.57, p50 11.6ms, max 1005ms, coherent but repetitive output)
Decision: regression > 3x, revert — DONE (file restored to original)

stories.title: Speed lever: ffn.slice_gate_up — replace 2 ttnn.slice with ttnn.split
stories.finding: NEUTRAL. Replacing 2 ttnn.slice with 1 ttnn.split call: decode_tps 74.08 vs baseline 74.35 (-0.27 t/s, within ~0.5 noise band). p50 = 11.6ms (same), max 89ms (marginal improvement). Speed-neutral means the two slices are ALREADY dispatched efficiently in trace mode. Reverted per L2 threshold (need >= +0.3 t/s). The 131us/call figure in non-trace profile is mostly host dispatch overhead absorbed by trace replay.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the 2 ttnn.slice calls with ttnn.split — DONE (then REVERTED)
bench_batch32 decode_tps tested — DONE (74.08 t/s, within noise)

stories.title: Commit PRD findings + run final verification bench
stories.finding: Both speed levers (L1 bfp8 KV cache, L2 ttnn.split) were tested and rejected: bfp8 catastrophic 3x regression, ttnn.split neutral within noise. No production-code commits from this cycle. PRD + progress.txt updated with two documented dead-end findings. Baseline re-bench on 7b282d8 shows decode_tps 74.35 t/s (healthy, above 70 floor).
acceptanceCriteria: Post-change bench — N/A (no code changes merged; ffn.py and generator_batch32.py reverted)
PCC vs HF from previous cycle still valid at 0.982045 (no code changed since K1 deslop)
PCC vs bitnet.cpp from previous cycle still valid at 0.9699
